data.title: Microservices
data.description: Google.com employs a microservices architecture allowing individual service components to scale independently.
data.technical_spec: Decoupled application architecture; deployment on Kubernetes.

data.title: Distributed Systems
data.description: Infrastructure spans globally across multiple data centers, supporting high availability and fault tolerance.
data.technical_spec: Use of Google Cloud Platform, Spanner, and Bigtable.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Advanced load balancing techniques are employed to handle millions of queries per second.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes Google Front End (GFE) and Cloud Load Balancers.

data.title: Caching
data.description: Intensive use of caching to improve response times and reduce server load.
data.technical_spec: Integration with edge caching systems like Varnish and proprietary cache solutions.

data.title: Search Indexing
data.description: Comprehensive indexing architecture to quickly fetch and display relevant results.
data.technical_spec: Utilizing Googlebot for web crawling and a massive inverted index for search.
